steps.title: Identify the author's stance
steps.content: The text argues that no single factor — including pedestrian amenities — fully explains pedestrian habits. Multiple factors (demographics, commute distance, etc.) all contribute, and replicating one feature won't transfer Vancouver's walkability to another city.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
Choice D matches: pedestrian amenities are just one of several contributing factors.
  • A claims the amenities reduce walkability elsewhere (unsupported)
  • B reverses cause and effect
  • C ranks demographics over amenities (the text says no factor dominates)
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
